hints.ablative: Ablative singular of 1st declension is also 'misericordia', but here it is the subject
hints.vocative: Vocative would be for direct address; 'misericordia' is the subject

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns end in -us/-um; 'misericordia' ends in -a
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has varied nominative endings; 'misericordia' follows 1st declension -a pattern
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns end in -es; 'misericordia' ends in -a

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'is' (masc.) or 'ea' (fem.); 'eius' is genitive

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'progenies' ends in -es with genitive 'progeniei'
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'progenies' has genitive 'progeniei'
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have -us genitive; 'progenies' has genitive 'progeniei'

translation: His mercy is from generation (to generation)
